text: Hello and welcome to This is my Architecture. Today I'm joined by Safar from HeyJobs.
Hey Safar. Hello David. Safar can you tell us about HeyJobs? At HeyJobs we are aiming to be
leading platform for those looking for the right job to live a fulfilling life. With our simple
search job at transparency and fast application process people can apply the jobs that matches
with their expectation in shortest possible time. Okay with that solution I'm sure you probably
process tons of data related to those jobs you display on the on the platform. Can you give me
a high level overview for the solution we're going to talk about today? Yeah every we have built a
serverless data solution every day we ingest three million records to our platform. How we do that?
We are pulling the data from partners. Partners providing endpoints to us and we are pulling the
data into our platform with cron jobs that we use with event bridge and then inside the lambda since
we receive huge data we are splitting into smaller chunks to be processed later in the platform. After
that after processing it what we are doing we are making it available in the talent platform for the
applications for search functionality for filtering too. Okay understand. So once you ingested this data
from the partner portals I assume this is like different formats different schemas how do you make
sure you have all the data normalized in your data platform? Yeah after ingesting that what we are doing
we are putting all the smaller chunks into SQS. We are saving them we are putting them into SQS and we can queue up
to process it later with not scaling too much too high and then we are consuming these SQS messages with lambda and
making normalizing them and making that the quality is high on that jobs. Also we are enriching the data too.
After that we are storing the data inside AuroraDB. Okay understand. So here each row in the database is basically
corresponding to one job that is later appearing on the talent platform right? Yes. Okay I also see a machine
learning component here at the bottom can you walk me through what that exactly does? Sometimes we are
receiving lean data from partners to enrich that data and categorize the data we are using machine learning
models that we created with SageMaker and also we are using natural language processing in that models
and after that we are communicating with the lambda that we use for processing and storing enriched data
inside the inside our database. Okay I understand how does the whole enrichment process here with machine
learning relate to the user experience in the end of the platform? At the end users can search with
better filters and they can filter base more granular way we have more options for them that we enrich the data.
Yeah okay that's that's great. Okay so you mentioned the talent platform now a few times what exactly does
it do and how do users in the end consume all of this information we have generated here? Yeah users are consuming
the data from the talent platform with a filtered and enriched data and then they are receiving it in a fine granular
ways. Okay and how does this data get to the talent platform? Yeah we are pushing a notification to SNS topic after we
process and reach the data and then SNS topic is fanning out the data into multiple SQS
SQS queues and after that this queue consumed by containers that we created inside talent platform and they are adding the data
to talent platform DB to make the data available for the customer users that we have. And the users basically consume it from
the web portal that is running here on this section. Yeah users are coming to our application and then with the front-end application
we are connecting to ECS containers and pulling the data for the users. Okay got it. I really like how you use
across the board serverless technologies you use messaging patterns here like this SNS and SQS chaining pattern here as well.
Can you walk me through the evolution of where you got to where you are at the moment? At first we had only this part
a monolith application that is serving ingesting doing all the job but it was hard to scale up and down for the load
that we have. What we have done we have built a serverless application that can scale up and down all the time.
Whatever we need we don't need to think about it if it is going to handle or not. Later on we have realized that from
few hundred thousands we can process few millions and then also we check the cost and we realize that
we saved up to 30 percent too. Okay that's quite impressive and that's the beauty of serverless right?
You only pay for the resources you consume. Yeah. I really like that. Thanks so much for sharing that Safa.
You're welcome. And thank you for watching This is my architecture.
